detail.tagline: A courtyard house on the studio's own street, built around a single fig tree.
narrative: Close to the studio's own door, this house wraps its rooms around a central courtyard rather than facing them to the street — a plan borrowed from the older houses of the town, reworked for a family of four.
A fig tree already growing on the site was kept and built around, rather than cleared for the foundations. Every principal room opens onto the courtyard it shades.
Deep reveals and a single continuous roof overhang keep the interior cool through summer without mechanical cooling in the living spaces.

gallery.alt: Courtyard with the retained fig tree
Living room opening to the courtyard
